issue_title: Is it possible to accumulate gradients over multiple batches?
issue_body: Due to the limit of GPU memory, I can not use a large mini-batch size when training a deep network like ResNet or Inception-v3. But I noticed that there is a solution in caffe that use the parameter 'iter_size' to accumulate gradients over multiple batches(i.e. accumulate gradients 10 times and then update the weights). So, is there a similar parameter setting in mxnet? If not, will it be easy to implement it myself?

Currently, when do global pooling in mxnet (with param.global_pool=True):
- input kernel size will be reset as input size (H, W);
- stride size will be reset to 1 for 1D, to (1, 1) for 2D;
- padding size will be set as what users input;
- output shape will be (N, C, 1) or (N, C, 1, 1) for 2D;

Please kindly find pooling code [here](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/src/operator/nn/pooling-inl.h#L101):
```
    pool(s, in_data[pool_enum::kData].dptr<DType>(),
         in_data[pool_enum::kData].shape_,
         out_data[pool_enum::kOut].shape_,
         param_.global_pool?
           TShape(ishape.data()+ishape.ndim()-param_.kernel.ndim(), ishape.data()+ishape.ndim())
           : param_.kernel,  // reset kernel size for global pooling
         param_.pad,  // padding size
         param_.global_pool? TShape(param_.kernel.ndim()) : param_.stride, // reset stride size
         param_.pool_type,
         req[pool_enum::kOut],
         out_data[pool_enum::kOut].dptr<DType>());
```

To my understanding, global pooling should aways be no padding or with padding size 0 and just do computation on the whole image or feature map. If user inputs a padding size (2, 2) to a global average pooling, I doubt that the current implementation will give a wrong output. (Maybe the result is the average of (-2, -2) to (H-2, W-2) in input image or feature map).

If global pooling with padding is a supported feature in mxnet, I think I need to change the code of mkldnn pooling to align with that. Otherwise, maybe we need change code to reset padding size to zero if a non-zero padding size is given to global pooling.

A simple test case is attached for your information.
[test_pool.txt](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/files/1699227/test_pool.txt)

issue_title: CSS for the Upload pattern is broken

issue_body:
Separating plone.app.event from plone.app.contenttypes did make sense: until 2.0 it contained Archetypes based Event type and Dexterity based behaviors, and this package is older than plone.app.contenttypes.

Since Archetypes support was removed with 2.0 we could merge plone.app.event into plone.app.contenttypes and simplify Plones dependency hell.

By the way, the event accessor pattern can also be removed, because that was only to support Archetypes and Dexterity with the same codebase.
And plone.event should be renamed to something not assuming to deal with an event notification system. Maybe plone.calendaring would be a better name.

Refer to this discussion: https://github.com/plone/Products.CMFPlone/issues/570

Issue #146 came about because RTD is set up to build master tip. So any time we merge changes, it updates the docs. Thus the docs can get out of sync with the released version.

This is compounded by two other issues:

1. after we do a release, we don't then update the version to 0.x.y.dev0 or something like that (I do that with my projects) -- if we did this, then the docs would show that they apply to a different version than the released one
2. after we do a release, we don't then update RTD to build docs for that tag as well (I do this with my projects sometimes) -- if we did this, then users would see that the docs they're looking at apply to a different version and can then switch to the version they want to see

The place in RTD to update versions is here:

https://readthedocs.org/projects/mozilla-django-oidc/

But only people who have access to that can do it.

This issue can be fixed entirely by adjusting the release process. I don't know if there's a good way to automate either of them because I do them by hand, but that's probably worth looking into.

I think this is worth fixing process-wise.

How do we want to deal with this?

in order to implement it for my own use, I read the source code. 
I guess there might be a logic bug -- unless I understand the idea in a wrong way..
I will try to explain it step by step.

during the _make_tree() step, a memory optimisation is used for nodes with n_descendants <= K, which means a sub-tree with only leave nodes can have a size as large as K.

**_get_all_nns()**, vector **nns** is used to store all potential candidates, and its size is limited as k * number_of_trees. where k is number of nearest neighbours to report.

at **_get_all_nns()**, the search for a tree stops when it meets a internal node with only leave nodes, and all of these leave nodes are added to vector **nns**. 

the problem is that vector **nns** quickly reaches it size limitation after searching for a few trees, and the majority of the forest is not visited.

In my test example, I build 500 trees for 2000 nodes, vectors has dimension of 400, and I want to find top 5 NN. **_get_all_nns()** only visits 10 trees and stops, leaving the remaining 490 trees untouched.
Maybe that explains why annoy works faster than many other programs...

I am not totally convinced that it is a heuristic instead of a bug in the program... 

Any comments are helpful.

issue_title: FBX importer crashes with zlib compiled with Z_SOLO
issue_body: If using an external zlib compiled with Z_SOLO, the FBX importer will crash decompressing a gzipped stream because it doesn't check the [return value of inflateInit](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/master/code/FBXParser.cpp#L552), which will fail with Z_SOLO defined and zstream.zalloc not set to a memory allocator.

issue_title: Collada Animations: Problem with the use of matrices
issue_body:
Hi folks

Internally, Assimp uses an aiMatrix4x4 to store rotations between animation keyframes, before later decomposing into a quaternion.

Unfortunately, quaternions and rotation matrices are only appropriate for representing orientation, and not rotation.

Here is the problem:

KEYFRAME
Time index: 0.0000000
RotateZ.ANGLE:  1080

KEYFRAME
Time index: 1.0000000
RotateZ.ANGLE:  1440

Because both 1080 and 1440 are multiples of 360, a rotation matrix and a quaternion treats them as zero, and no animation occurs.

For the purposes of animation, we need to represent *rotation* and NOT just *orientation*.

In order to repair this issue, I believe that we need to stop using quaternions and the internal matrix to handle rotations in keyframes.

Instead, perhaps AxisAngle notation could be used?

Here is a test model which exhibits this problem:
https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/u/29850039/Array%20Test%20001.zip
Do wee need the Assimp.Net first?

issue_title: Make the entire importer/exporter API externally available
issue_body: Currently as of 4.1, Assimp only makes the bare minimum importer/exporter API available (BaseImporter, ai* types, etc). Many of the convenience APIs (ByteSwapper, StreamReader, IrrXML, CommentRemover, ComputeNormalsWithSmoothingsGroups(), SkeletonMeshBuilder, StandardShapes, fast_atof, StringComparison, ParsingUtils, and perhaps others) don't have their headers installed, nor can they be called from the library by external code, leaving me to have to turn to libraries like GLM and Boost more often, to help extract various format data, when it would be easier to use what Assimp already provides internally.

I have met this issue: "AttributeError: 'Connection' object has no attribute 'readonly'" when query tables names. So I have analyzed your source. Based on my analysis of it, I have found one issue.
'Connection' class is inherited from Database but it does not inherit parent's method or attributes from Database class.
To fix it, I have added a following code.
```sh
super(Connection, self).__init__(db_name, db_url, username, password)
```
After that, it works for me well.

This GMPE has methods instantiating subclasses of it. This breaks the engine, since instantiating a GSIM on the worker nodes has been forbidden (the motivation is to make sure that the GSIMs
are instantiated on the controller node, where they can have access to the file system).
The solution is to convert the affected methods to class methods that do not require instantiation. They should have been classmethods from the beginning.

issue_title: Store in the source_info also the number of tasks
issue_body: We are storing now the cumulative time but not the number of tasks working on a given source. Also, we should store the maximum calculation time.

New scripts added must have a shebang pointing to python3:

`#!/usr/bin/env python3`

1. we are not compatible with python2 anymore
2. packaging systems like `rpm` check the shebangs and add dependencies automatically, so having wrong shebangs will add unwanted dependencies

Existing shebangs are fixed by #3889 but we need to be careful with new scripts!

issue_title: How can i get the Upfront or PartialUpfront price paid for a Reserved Instance thru boto3
issue_body:
I am trying to capture the total cost of a Reserved Instance. Through boto3, i am able to get the Recurring Price of an instance, but not the Upfront or the Partial Upfront paid. How can i get that.

`
>>> import boto3
>>> c = boto3.client('ec2', region_name='us-east-1')
>>> rsv = c.describe_reserved_instances().get('ReservedInstances')
>>> for r in rsv:
...    if r['State'] == 'active':
...        print r
...        break
... 
{u'ReservedInstancesId': '99-dummy-id-29', u'OfferingType': 'Partial Upfront', u'AvailabilityZone': 'us-east-1e', u'End': datetime.datetime(2016, 8, 12, 20, 51, 13, tzinfo=tzutc()), u'ProductDescription': 'Linux/UNIX (Amazon VPC)', u'UsagePrice': 0.0, u'RecurringCharges': [{u'Amount': 0.0247, u'Frequency': 'Hourly'}], u'Start': datetime.datetime(2015, 8, 14, 5, 0, tzinfo=tzutc()), u'State': 'active', u'FixedPrice': 0.0, u'CurrencyCode': 'USD', u'Duration': 31536000, u'InstanceTenancy': 'default', u'InstanceType': 'c3.large', u'InstanceCount': 4}`

#### Issues
Subnet is one of the most important part of VPC design. However, one cannot give Subnet a name to the subnet, for subsequent resource creation usage.

Current boto3 does not allow one to assign a name nor tag name during subnet creations. 
```
response = client.create_subnet(
    DryRun=True|False,
    VpcId='string',
    CidrBlock='string',
    AvailabilityZone='string'
)
```
It is very useful if given a subnet name , e.g. 

```
response = client.create_subnet(
    DryRun=True|False,
    SubnetName='string',
    VpcId='string',
    CidrBlock='string',
    AvailabilityZone='string'
)

### in fact , create_security_group make GroupName as compulsory
response = client.create_security_group(
    DryRun=True|False,
    GroupName='string',
    Description='string',
    VpcId='string'
)
```

#### Reasons
A subnet Name or a enforce tag name is very useful for admin to launch AWS services and attach to proper VPC Subnet and security group, using implicit naming than explicit ID. 

For example , the current create_db_subnet_group()  , one must enter explicit SubnetIDs  to create a db_subnet_group.
```
response = client.create_db_subnet_group(
    DBSubnetGroupName='string',
    DBSubnetGroupDescription='string',
    SubnetIds=[
        'string',
    ],
    Tags=[
        {
            'Key': 'string',
            'Value': 'string'
        },
    ]
)
```

IMHO, it will make life easier extend to use subnet name if the features is extended. 
```
response = client.create_db_subnet_group(
    DBSubnetGroupName='string',
    DBSubnetGroupDescription='string',
    SubnetIds=[
        'string',
    ],
    SubnetName=[
        'string',
    ],
    Tags=[
        {
            'Key': 'string',
            'Value': 'string'
        },
    ]
)
```

#### Proposed SubnetName  features  usage example
```
create_subnet(
    SubnetName='Web-subnet-us-1a',
    VpcId= "vpc-12345678",  
    CidrBlock='172.16.10.0/24',
    AvailabilityZone = "us-east-1a")

create_subnet(
    SubnetName='Web-subnet-us-1b',
    VpcId= "vpc-12345678",  
    CidrBlock='172.16.11.0/24',
    AvailabilityZone = "us-east-1b")

#### subsequent script don't need to quest the subnet-id, i.e. 
create_db_subnet_group(
  DBSubnetGroupName='web-mysql-sbgn',
  SubnetName=['Web-subnet-us-1a', 'Web-subnet-us-1b'] 
)

```

#### Current workaround
To insert subnet-id into boto3 /aws cli resource creation script, it is a tedious script workflow in boto3  
```
improt boto3
ec2=boto3.client("ec2")
# 1. get response ASAP from create_subnet 
subnet_response = ec2.create_subnet( ....) 

# 2. Attach the tag 
tag_list= [{ 'Key' : 'Name',
    'Value' : 'Web-subnet-us-1a'}] 

ec2.create_tags(
  Resources=[subnet_response["Subnet"]["SubnetId"]],
  Tags= tag_list
)

# 3. All resources using the subnet now may use describe_subnets() to find it

subnet_filter= [{'Name':'tag:Name', 'Values': [ "Web-subnet-us-1a" ]}]
required_subnet_id =  ec2.describe_subnet(Filters=subnet_filter) 

```

issue_title: Allow alphabetization of logged request dicts
issue_body:
I spend a lot of time looking at log messages from boto3.resources.action that include a complete dynamodb request, represented as JSON, like this:
```
boto3.resources.action [DEBUG] Calling dynamodb:update_item with {'ExpressionAttributeNames': {'#status': 'status'}, 'UpdateExpression': 'set #status = :status', 'TableName': 'things', 'Key': {'uuid': 'da1a6ba8-bec5-4eb6-b2ce-27b7d5c69459', 'thing_id': }, 'ExpressionAttributeValues': {':status': 'FLAKY'}}
```
In fact, my logs have zillions of lines like these.  I've noticed that the ordering of the items in the request can vary; i.e, in addition to the line above, I might also see a line like
```
boto3.resources.action [DEBUG] Calling dynamodb:update_item with {'ExpressionAttributeValues': {':mo_status': 'BADNEWS', }, 'UpdateExpression': 'SET #status = :mo_status', 'Key': {'uuid': '22a9a4a4-cae3-4783-ab4a-b71f79e162fe'}, 'ConditionExpression': 'attribute_exists(uuid)', 'ExpressionAttributeNames': {'#status': 'status'}, 'TableName': 'wassup'}
```
Obviously I've made up most of that data for this example, but one thing that I did not make up, and which is my point, is that in the first line, `ExpressionAttributeNames` comes before `ExpressionAttributeValues`, whereas in the second line, the order is reversed.  
> I am guessing that this comes from the hash randomization that was added in python 3.3 (my code uses python 3.4)

What I'd like is some way to tell boto to alphabetize those keys when logging, so that the log lines look more similar than they do now; I expect this will make it easier for me to spot anomalies when quickly scanning a huge log file by eye.

I confess that I haven't thought about how I'd want the UI feature for this to look.  I'd guess you wouldn't want to enable this all the time, since it would add overhead that most people don't need.

issue_title: Confusing behavior when overwriting previous mocks/stubs
issue_body:
It seems that more specific stubs/mocks always have precedence over more generic ones, which has caused me some amount of pain and confusion. 

In my test suites, I use the following pattern a lot, where I set up default behavior for the entire function-in-test during setup, and then only vary portions of the function-in-test during each individual test method. However, there are multiple times where I have encountered the following situation:

```python
class TestStuff(object):
    def setup(self):
        allow(Module).foo.with_args('baz').and_return('giraffe')

    def test_something(self):
        allow(Module).foo.and_return('hippo')
        Module.foo('baz')  # Looks like it should return 'hippo', but will return 'giraffe'
```

Is this expected behavior? (It seems to me like a behavior that would make more sense is that mocks/stubs should be tried in order from most recent until earliest, until one works.) If this is the intended behavior, can it be documented? Either way, can we add a function that will let me clear all mocks/stubs from a method and return it to normal, so I can be sure that only the mocks/stubs specified after the 'clear' will be in effect?

issue_title: Using a @list variable in an Execute Javascript call doesn't work
issue_body:
Using a scalar variable in `Execute Javascript` code works fine, but list variables break. For example this code:

```
@{USERS}              ADMIN                TEST

Execute Javascript    $('#users .multiselect').multiselect('select', '@USERS')
```
produces this error:
```
Executing JavaScript:
$('#users .multiselect').multiselect('select', '[u'ADMIN', u'TEST']')
```

This is because the Python list is getting converted into a string - by python - and it's including the `u` unicode indicators - which means that Javascript doesn't recognise this as a list.

Doing something like this instead works ok:

```
${USERS}    '["ADMIN","TEST"]'

Execute Javascript    $('#users .multiselect').multiselect('select', '$USERS')
```

I've really liked that you can specify optional and suggested packages in composer and I wish to suggest that functionality for this really great tool:

The optional packages usually don't require for the project to run or develop but they could enhance the experience mostly for development but some cases to the environment as well. A good example could be: `django-debug-toolbar`, `ipython`, the optional packages for `djangorestframework`. I'm sure there is more but I just only used django in my first 6 month so far :smile: 

Suggested packages are a subtype of the optional packages, they just a hook to make a note after you firstly installed the project from the really important options.

Both options behave just like the `[dev-packages]` but with a different flag.

Thoughts, Ideas, Opinions are welcomed!

It may be handful to proxy some other pip commands to work with pipenv.

## Freeze

##### Expected result

Resolve and freeze all dependencies from Pipfile to "requirements.txt format".

```
$ pipenv freeze --dev
requests==2.18.4
```

##### Current workaround

Requires installation before freezing.

```
$ pipenv install --dev
$ pipenv run pip freeze
requests==2.18.4
```

## Download

##### Expected result

Resolve and download all packages from Pipfile to some destination directory.

```
$ pipenv download --dev -d ./dest
```

##### Current workaround

Requires installation and freezing before downloading.

```
$ pipenv install --dev
$ pipenv run pip freeze > requirements.txt
$ pipenv run pip download -d ./dest -r requirements.txt
```

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Given how `pipenv` uses a verb command interface, it feels wrong that to get a help/command summary you need to invoke it with `--help` instead of `pipenv help`.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Enable `pipenv help` to work. Can be shared with the `--help` output. This maps better in terms of usability.

If okay, I can put a PR together for this.

##### Issue description

I'm on a Mac and installed `pre-commit` using `brew` and without even asking for it got upgraded from python 3.6.5 to 3.7.  (3.6.5 was also under the control of `brew`)

Now pipenv fails with "Library not loaded"-error.

##### Expected result

Pipenv to work as before the python upgrade.

##### Actual result

```
$ pipenv -h
dyld: Library not loaded: @executable_path/../.Python
  Referenced from: /usr/local/Cellar/pipenv/2018.5.18/libexec/bin/python3.6
  Reason: image not found
Abort trap: 6
```

##### Comments

1. What's the proper way of fixing the issue? Can I edit some config-file to point to 3.7 instead?

2. Could `pipenv` perhaps be more lenient and rely on the `python3` binary instead of the `python3.6`?

issue_title: Pipenv error unclear when attempting system install as non-root in docker
issue_body:
I am attempting to install in a Docker container, and I was getting this error:

Step 6/10 : RUN pipenv install --system --deploy
Warning: --system is intended to be used for pre-existing Pipfile installation, not installation of specific packages. Aborting.
The command '/bin/sh -c pipenv install --system --deploy' returned a non-zero code: 1

The error message make think there was an issue with my Pipfile.lock.  I rebuilt the lock file, and it did not help.  

I was able to resolve the issue by changing my user to root and not attempting to do it in the root directoyr.  

Please run `$ python -m k.help`, and paste the results here.

If you're on MacOS, just run the following:

    $ python -m pipenv.help | pbcopy

------------

##### Expected result

I expected an error message to tell me that I did not have permissions or something to that effect.

I'd be happy to work on a pull request to help with this, if it is something that would be useful.

issue_title: Pipenv update does not update library when GitHub ref in Pipfile is updated
issue_body:
First of all, thanks for improving Python packaging. I'm experimenting with Pipenv on some side projects before bringing it into my team's mainline dev workflow.

##### Brief description
`$ pipenv update` does not update library when a ref is changed in the Pipfile

Pipfile Snippet 1:
`python-binance = {git = "git://github.com/sammchardy/python-binance.git", ref = "e1be0e17921965c1d34e8dbccac522e55fa0f4ac"}`

Pipfile Snippet 2:
`python-binance = {git = "git://github.com/sammchardy/python-binance.git", ref = "d35b2bb0e1c4cd4d8b9042fc130d82c041fe3c3c"}`

------------

##### Expected result

I assumed that by changing the ref in `Pipfile` to a different commit hash and running `$ pipenv update` that the old verison of the library would have been removed and the new version would have been installed.

```
# Expected result:
$ pip list | grep binance 
python-binance        0.6.6
```

##### Actual result
The old version stayed in place.
```
# Actual result:
$ pip list | grep binance 
python-binance        0.6.3
```

##### Steps to replicate

```
# With old Pipfile:
$ pipenv update
...
$ pip list | grep binance 
python-binance        0.6.3

# With new Pipfile:
$ pipenv update
...
$ pip list | grep binance
python-binance        0.6.3
```

issue_body:
My ubuntu has an egg-info that pkg_resources fails to read with a UnicodeDecodeError. This made all sorts of things fail in my virtual environment.

```
> git clone https://github.com/pypa/setuptools
...
> cd setuptools/
> python -c "import pkg_resources"
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2964, in <module>
    @_call_aside
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2950, in _call_aside
    f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2977, in _initialize_master_working_set
    working_set = WorkingSet._build_master()
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 626, in _build_master
    ws = cls()
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 619, in __init__
    self.add_entry(entry)
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 675, in add_entry
    for dist in find_distributions(entry, True):
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 1988, in find_on_path
    path_item, entry, metadata, precedence=DEVELOP_DIST
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2376, in from_location
    py_version=py_version, platform=platform, **kw
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2717, in _reload_version
    md_version = _version_from_file(self._get_metadata(self.PKG_INFO))
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2341, in _version_from_file
    line = next(iter(version_lines), '')
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2509, in _get_metadata
    for line in self.get_metadata_lines(name):
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 1879, in get_metadata_lines
    return yield_lines(self.get_metadata(name))
  File "pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 1869, in get_metadata
    metadata = f.read()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/codecs.py", line 296, in decode
    (result, consumed) = self._buffer_decode(data, self.errors, final)
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0xb6 in position 147: invalid start byte in /usr/lib/pymodules/python2.7/rpl-1.5.5.egg-info
```

The problematic egg-info file is attached, with an added extension (txt) to fool github into accepting the attachment.
[rpl-1.5.5.egg-info.txt](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/files/401454/rpl-1.5.5.egg-info.txt)

This is with python 2.7.2 and setuptools aad4a6913d3e505064c76322d20756909724c411.

To make my virtualenv work properly, I had to patch pkg_resources/__init__.py, replacing the line:

```
        md_version = _version_from_file(self._get_metadata(self.PKG_INFO))
```
in EggInfoDistribution._reload_version with this:

```
        try: 
            md_version = _version_from_file(self._get_metadata(self.PKG_INFO))
        except UnicodeDecodeError as e: 
            warnings.warn(
                'failure to read version number of %s at %s due to: %s' % (self.project_name, self.location, e))
            md_version = None
```

I can make a pull request out of this if you think this is a good solution.

BTW, the stack trace looks similar to that of #531 . You can also see multiple other people running into the same problem on stackoverflow.com, [here](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/34440958/cant-run-pip-unicodedecodeerror), [here](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/36684405/pip-in-crunchbang-error-unicodedecodeerror-utf8-codec-cant-decode-byte-0xb), and [here](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/35427548/unicodedecodeerror-utf8-codec-cant-decode-byte-0x92-in-position-661-invalid).

issue_title: api: add optional headers dict to put_object, fput_object, and copy_object
issue_body:
Many tools support S3 storage classes.  HashBackup does.  To do that, a header needs to be included in put and copy requests:
```
headers = {'x-amz-storage-class': 'STANDARD_IA'}
```
This can't really be done in the constructor when creating the client, because within a bucket, files can be in different storage classes.  When uploading small files less than 52K, Infrequent Access is actually more expensive than Standard storage because for IA, Amazon always charges for at least 128K.

I can take a look at doing this if it would be acceptable.

The change would be:
- add optional headers dict to put/fput/copy object apis, default is {}
- for small objects, pass the header dict to _do_put_object
-- in _do_put_object, add the Content-Length, Content-Type, and Content-Md5 headers to the header dict instead of creating a new dict
- for large objects, pass headers to _do_put_multipart_object; the headers go in the Initiate Multipart Upload request.  Looks a little messier, but same concept
- copy_object already has a header-like argument, "conditions".  It's just a class over a header dictionary.

Looks like 2 ways to do this:

1. add some new methods to CopyConditions, like set_storage_class and include a conditions arg in put_object and fput_object, like copy_object
-or-
2. add header arg to all 3.  This would be a little more flexible, because any header could be added, not just storage class.  And storage class is not exactly a "copy condition", even though very similar.

issue_title: "plating" decorator for quickly constructing simple sites
issue_body:
Right now it's a bit tedious to construct a Klein site with the usual accoutrements of a simple website; some consistent navigation and page chrome, different things in the content area of each page, et cetera.

I propose that we add a decorator that assists in the construction of such sites, making it easy to quickly type some templates in-line, then gradually move them to external XML templates as they become more complex.

The decorator I propose decorates functions which return dictionaries of slot-filling data, so the decoratee's job is simply to populate the correct model data and the *decorator's* job is to fill out the `Element` with the appropriate twisted.web.template stuff to make it render HTML.

This is related to #78, in that ideally, the model data returned by the function could be returned as serialized JSON if the client asks for JSON, and only converted into HTML with styling if the client is a browser asking for HTML.

Prototype will be attached below.

No exec file in '/lib/python2.7/site-packages/scrapyd-client', only 'scrapyd-deploy' file in it.

Command 'scrapyd-deploy' runs OK. Has 'scrapyd-client' been removed?

When installed on a virtual environment, I needed to add  a scrapyd-deploy.bat file in scripts folder in order for it to be recognized by the active environment

One should expect that after pip installed the package you shouldn't need to make any fixtures to use the tool

Regards

issue_title: Wrong name for partition_key attribute in documentation
issue_body:
The documentation only makes reference of an optional "key" attribute in messages, but I think the attribute is called partition_key
The following code prints message keys correctly (while msg.key give an `AttributeError` all the times)

```
while True:
    msg = balanced_consumer.consume()
    try :
       print msg.partition_key
       print msg.value
    except AttributeError:
       print msg.value
```

Maybe it should be explained in pykafka/pykafka/protocol.py https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/c2e9c5e9ba5fce10405dfb3c5d62636b1d7415c6/pykafka/protocol.py#L139

Thanks

First: kudos on a fine effort here. Been following along for a little while and I look forward to the next release. :beers: 

I tried out the balancedconsumer this evening and while it consumes, the balancing appears not to work as I'd expect (expectations on the matter having been set by the Java high level consumer). Here's my dumb little consumer https://github.com/benauthor/storm-env/blob/master/consumers/pykafka-balanced.py. I've got a dumb little producer producing round-robin to a topic of say 6 partitions.

Expected behavior:
- Start consumer A on topic & group. It consumes messages from all 6 partitions.
- Start consumer B on topic & group. B gets 3 partitions, and A is rebalanced to 3.
- Kill consumer B. A rebalanced again and consumes all 6 partitions.

- Start consumer A on topic & group. It consume messages from all 6 partitions.
- Start consumer B on topic & group. B gets 3 partitions. A stalls, stops consuming messages.

issue_title: Allow non-uberjar deploys for speed & simplicity
issue_body:
We currently have `lein uberjar` running in these lines:

https://github.com/Parsely/streamparse/blob/master/streamparse/ext/invoke.py#L135-L146

That makes the JAR file contain Storm + all its JAR dependencies, which explodes the topology JAR size and makes deploys slow. A Python codebase that is only 20-30KB ends up being a 15MB JAR to submit.

The non-uberjar is built (it's the one without `-standalone`) but we are uploading the uberjar.

issue_title: analogous parameter settings for aperture_photometry and iraf.phot?
issue_body:
Hi,
Have there been any tests done to compare the ``aperture_photometry`` function to ``iraf.phot``?  Or is there a chart about how to set parameters so that the two get ~matching results?  
I have been finding discrepancies in flux counts on the same sources up to 20% or more in circular apertures radii of 3-20pixels (the range I have so far look at) no matter my combination of setting centroiding on or off in ``iraf.phot`` or setting subpixels in ``aperture_photometry``.  Any help would be great; I need to provide an explanation for the discrepancy if I am to justify to my team switching the photometry scripts over from iraf.  It's not clear to me what is going on under the hood of ``aperture_photometry``.

issue_title: Can't update French Connect Custom account's legal entity with a new account token
issue_body:
Seems like a bug in the library as I can make it work with curl, see code below

```python
    legal_entity_data = { 'first_name': 'something' }
    account_token = stripe.Token.create( account = { 'legal_entity': legal_entity_data, 'tos_shown_and_accepted': True })
    stripe_account = stripe.Account.create(type = 'custom', country = 'FR', account_token = account_token)

    account_token = stripe.Token.create( account = { 'legal_entity': legal_entity_data, 'tos_shown_and_accepted': True })

issue_title: Allow Fireworks to reference data held in Workflow
issue_body:
I run a lot of workflows where I have a template which I modify for each individual Firework in a Workflow.  This leads to a lot of bloat in the Launchpad (ie the mongodb).

Ideally I'd be allowed to do something like this:
```python
fw1 = fw.Firework([Task1(), Task2()], fw_spec={'template': WorkflowStatic('template')})
fw2 = fw.Firework([Task1(), Task2()], fw_spec={'template': WorkflowStatic('template')})

fw.Workflow([fw1,fw2], statics={'template': [1, 2, 3, 4]})
```
So `template` is defined once in the Workflow, and Fireworks can refer to it in their spec.

In terms of implementing this, after a quick poke around, I think I could do it by changing `Launchpad.get_fw_dict_by_id` to scan for the `WorkflowStatic` objects and replace these with the corresponding document from the Workflow.

So questions..
 - has this been thought of before? (and is it a bad idea?)
 - is it enough to modify `get_fw_dict_by_id` or are there other points that a Firework is accessed?

I've created an improved [Robust matching using RANSAC](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/auto_examples/plot_matching.html) example which warps and overlays the robust estimate on the image. This highlighted an important inconsistency (bug) in the results of the Affine Transform estimate function. The warp matrix estimate is neither the correct matrix or its inverse. Instead, it is a permutation of the correct desired output matrix. The problem is illustrated very clearly in the following image:

![skimage_bug](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/55744/10534327/df95ec80-73a4-11e5-9b81-82b1d5a18d58.png)

The code to reproduce the issue is below, you may want to consider incorporating it into the original example since it is an improved demo:

```python
"""
============================
Robust matching using RANSAC
============================

In this simplified example we first generate two synthetic images as if they
were taken from different view points.

In the next step we find interest points in both images and find
correspondences based on a weighted sum of squared differences of a small
neighborhood around them. Note, that this measure is only robust towards
linear radiometric and not geometric distortions and is thus only usable with
slight view point changes.

After finding the correspondences we end up having a set of source and
destination coordinates which can be used to estimate the geometric
transformation between both images. However, many of the correspondences are
faulty and simply estimating the parameter set with all coordinates is not
sufficient. Therefore, the RANSAC algorithm is used on top of the normal model
to robustly estimate the parameter set by detecting outliers.

"""
from __future__ import print_function

import numpy as np
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt

from skimage import data
from skimage.util import img_as_float
from skimage.feature import (corner_harris, corner_subpix, corner_peaks,
                             plot_matches)
from skimage.transform import warp, AffineTransform
from skimage.exposure import rescale_intensity
from skimage.color import rgb2gray
from skimage.measure import ransac
from skimage  import io

original=True

# warp synthetic image
tform = AffineTransform(scale=(0.9, 0.9), rotation=0.2, translation=(20, -10))
    
if original:
    # generate synthetic checkerboard image and add gradient for the later matching
    checkerboard = img_as_float(data.checkerboard())
    img_orig = np.zeros(list(checkerboard.shape) + [3])
    img_orig[..., 0] = checkerboard
    gradient_r, gradient_c = (np.mgrid[0:img_orig.shape[0],
                                       0:img_orig.shape[1]]
                              / float(img_orig.shape[0]))
    img_orig[..., 1] = gradient_r
    img_orig[..., 2] = gradient_c
    img_orig = rescale_intensity(img_orig)
    img_orig_gray = rgb2gray(img_orig)
    
    img_warped = warp(img_orig, tform.inverse, output_shape=(200, 200))
    img_warped_gray = rgb2gray(img_warped)
else:
    imageName='test1.png'
    imageName2='test2.png'
    
    #print "imageName:",imageName
    #print "imageName2:",imageName2
    
    
    img1  = io.imread(imageName)
    img2  = io.imread(imageName2)
    
    img_orig        = img1
    img_warped      = img2
    img_orig_gray   = rgb2gray(img_orig)
    img_warped_gray = rgb2gray(img_warped)
    

# extract corners using Harris' corner measure
coords_orig = corner_peaks(corner_harris(img_orig_gray), threshold_rel=0.001,
                           min_distance=5)
coords_warped = corner_peaks(corner_harris(img_warped_gray),
                             threshold_rel=0.001, min_distance=5)

# determine sub-pixel corner position
coords_orig_subpix = corner_subpix(img_orig_gray, coords_orig, window_size=9)
coords_warped_subpix = corner_subpix(img_warped_gray, coords_warped,
                                     window_size=9)


def gaussian_weights(patch_radius, sigma=1):
    y, x = np.mgrid[-patch_radius:patch_radius+1, -patch_radius:patch_radius+1]
    g = np.zeros(y.shape, dtype=np.double)
    g[:] = np.exp(-0.5 * (x**2 / sigma**2 + y**2 / sigma**2))
    g /= 2 * np.pi * sigma * sigma
    return g



def match_corner(coord_list1,coord_list2,img1,img2, patch_radius=5):#     # find correspondences using simple weighted sum of squared differences
    
    c1len = len(coord_list1)
    c2len = len(coord_list2)
    cl1index = np.arange(c1len)
    cl2index = np.arange(c2len)
    src = []
    dst = []
    for [[r,c],idx] in zip(coord_list1,cl1index):
        src.append(idx)
        window_orig = img1[r-patch_radius:r+patch_radius+1,
                          c-patch_radius:c+patch_radius+1]
    
        # weight pixels depending on distance to center pixel
        weights = gaussian_weights(patch_radius, 3)
    
        # compute sum of squared differences to all corners in warped image
        SSDs = []
        for cr, cc in coord_list2:
            window_warped = img2[cr-patch_radius:cr+patch_radius+1,
                                 cc-patch_radius:cc+patch_radius+1]
            SSD = np.sum(weights * (window_orig - window_warped)**2)
            SSDs.append(SSD)
    
        # use corner with minimum SSD as correspondence
        min_idx = np.argmin(SSDs)
        dst.append(min_idx)
    
   
    return [np.array(coord_list1[src]),np.array(coord_list2[dst])]


# find correspondences using simple weighted sum of squared differences
[src,dst] = match_corner(coords_orig,coords_warped,img_orig_gray,img_warped_gray, patch_radius=5)



# estimate affine transform model using all coordinates
model = AffineTransform()
model.estimate(src, dst)

# robustly estimate affine transform model with RANSAC
model_robust, inliers = ransac((src, dst), AffineTransform, min_samples=8,
                               residual_threshold=2, max_trials=10000)
outliers = inliers == False


# compare "true" and estimated transform parameters
print(tform.scale, tform.translation, tform.rotation)
print(model.scale, model.translation, model.rotation)
print(model_robust.scale, model_robust.translation, model_robust.rotation)

# visualize correspondence
fig, ax = plt.subplots(nrows=4, ncols=1)

def warp_and_combine(img1,img2,xform):
    img1_warped = warp(img1,xform)
    img1f = img1_warped.astype(float)*0.5
    img2f = img_warped_gray.astype(float)*0.5
    if img1f.shape == img2f.shape:
        combined = img1f+img2f
    else:
        combined = img1f
    
    return combined
        


plt.gray()

inlier_idxs = np.nonzero(inliers)[0]
plot_matches(ax[0], img_orig_gray, img_warped_gray, src, dst,
             np.column_stack((inlier_idxs, inlier_idxs)), matches_color='b')
ax[0].axis('off')
ax[0].set_title('Correct correspondences')

outlier_idxs = np.nonzero(outliers)[0]
plot_matches(ax[1], img_orig_gray, img_warped_gray, src, dst,
             np.column_stack((outlier_idxs, outlier_idxs)), matches_color='r')
ax[1].axis('off')
ax[1].set_title('Faulty correspondences')

ax[2].axis('off')
ax[2].set_title('inverse transform overlay')
ax[2].imshow(warp_and_combine(img_orig_gray,img_warped_gray,model_robust.inverse))


ax[3].axis('off')
ax[3].set_title('transform overlay')
ax[3].imshow(warp_and_combine(img_orig_gray,img_warped_gray,model_robust))

plt.show()

```

I use example code provided by skimage in this [link](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/auto_examples/xx_applications/plot_thresholding.html#sphx-glr-auto-examples-xx-applications-plot-thresholding-py) in Anaconda.

But I have an error:

`ImportError: cannot import name 'try_all_threshold'`

Other modules in `skimage.filters` have no problems.

Python 3.5.2 |Anaconda custom (x86_64)
skimage 0.12.3

```patch
diff --git a/requirements.txt b/requirements.txt
index fe3dddd..a5941a3 100644
--- a/requirements.txt
+++ b/requirements.txt
@@ -17,5 +17,5 @@ pyproj==1.9.3
 prettytable==0.7.2
 # When updating nupic.bindings, also update any shared dependencies to keep
 # versions in sync.
-nupic.bindings==0.4.6
+nupic.bindings>=0.4.6
 numpy==1.9.2
```

I believe fixing this will require modifications to release process in both projects.

issue_title: Update CONTRIBUTING.md with complete info from wiki
The "custom region demo" doesn't do what it says it does
issue_body:
There are a couple of issues in:

/examples/network/custom_region/identity_region.py
/examples/network/custom_region_demo.py

## Issue 1

It says it passes "the encoded representation to an Identity Region". But it actually passes the unencoded input. 

This line uses the wrong source on the RecordSensor:

~~~python
  network.link("sensor", "identityRegion", "UniformLink", "",
               srcOutput="sourceOut", destInput="in")
~~~

It should use srcOutput "dataOut", or just use the default.

~~~python
  network.link("sensor", "identityRegion", "UniformLink", "")
~~~

## Issue 2

The IdentityRegion is not actually writing to the output. It's reassigning the output variable to reference a totally different array.

The fix: Change

~~~python
outputs["out"] = inputs["in"]
~~~

to

~~~python
outputs["out"][:] = inputs["in"]
~~~


## Issue 3

Now that we've solved those, we need to put the correct `dataWidth` in the IdentityRegion.

Basically, change the line:

~~~python
network.addRegion("identityRegion", "py.IdentityRegion", json.dumps(I_PARAMS))
~~~

to:

~~~python
network.addRegion("identityRegion", "py.IdentityRegion", json.dumps({
        "dataWidth": sensor.encoder.getWidth(),
    }))
~~~

issue_title: Runtime Error CHECK FAILED: "found" in Connections.cpp
Solve stdc++ ABI incompatibility between manylinux nupic.bindings and recent Linux distros
issue_body:
It turns out that the c\+\+11 standard prescribed a new ABI for certain classes, but compiler toolchains took their own sweet time to implement that ABI. While they support -std=c\+\+11, the implementation of the new ABI is not uniform. The baseline C++ toolchain prescribed by the manylinux policy supports std=c\+\+11, but doesn’t implement the new ABI. The 5.4.0 toolchain on Ubuntu 16.04 implements the new ABI, which alters declarations of some standard classes, and exceptions in particular.

One area where this impacts us is in the ability to catch some exceptions. For example, https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/b9247a270e2fae2f4586286163b6472a909560e2/src/nupic/regions/VectorFile.cpp#L177 catches ios_base::failure&. This is one of the exceptions impacted by the ABI change. In the manylinux toolchain, it’s simply derived from std::exception, which is the signature that gets compiled into nupic.core when building a manylinux wheel. However, the new ABI mandates that it should be derived from std::system_error (and sets some additional attributes), which gives it a different signature. Thus, our manylinux build is unable to catch that exception when running on a system whose stdc\+\+ library incorporates the new ABI (e.g. Ubuntu 16.04). There is a discussion about it in https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=66145.

For this specific case, the workaround may be to catch std::exception instead of or in addition to ios_base::failure or to restructure the use of streaming API to not make use of exceptions. However, the vast body of 3rd party code in nupic.core also makes use of this specific exception,  and the ABI change may impact some other classes, likely making it impractical to make such adaptations in the overall vast code base.

Another possibility that may be more practical:
1. Include libstdc++ statically
2. And use a version script in the builds of the nupic.bindings extension shared libs that hides all symbols except the extension initialization functions.
 * When libstdc\+\+ links statically, it's symbol references still show up as public in the generated extension shared libs. This means that if something else in the application (e.g., pycapnp) causes the shared libstdc\+\+ to be loaded, then the C\+\+ references in our extensions might be linked to the shared libstdc\+\+ that implements the new ABI instead of the statically-linked libstdc\+\+.

Invenio 1.1.6

Following up on https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/issues/2921 I have come to the conclusion that our extension code could be simplified by dropping support for ``LESS_RUN_IN_DEBUG``.

This would avoid, for instance, the following 'hack' that needed to be introduced https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/pull/2922/files#diff-dc717d85d0b109c334416b4b5a305f8cR136

What this variable does is enabling compilation of less files in the browser http://lesscss.org/#client-side-usage

But this does not offer any advantage as far as I can see.

By default ``webassets`` will do the right thing and if a ``less`` file on a Bundle is modified, upon page refresh, the bundle is recompiled and changes are visible immediately. If there are no changes then no compilation happens.

This is a WIP branch where I show what the change would be https://github.com/jmartinm/invenio/commit/5fdd9c0a540d2f8947dd81639acb5325dcc62e72

Proposal
=======

* [x] Drop support for LESS_RUN_IN_DEBUG (See [this commit](https://github.com/jmartinm/invenio/commit/5fdd9c0a540d2f8947dd81639acb5325dcc62e72)) (PR #3001)
* [ ] Enable less source maps (http://robdodson.me/debug-less-with-chrome-developer-tools/) - no less.js needed
* [ ] Fix ``depends=`` parameter used, for example, [here](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/invenio/base/bundles.py#L80) so that we can have imports in the ``less`` code like [here](https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-next/blob/master/inspire/base/static/less/inspire.less) and a change on any of the dependent files will trigger recompilation of ``less``. This is currently not happening, likely due to something missing in our [custom resolver](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/invenio/ext/assets/extensions.py#L193)

I think this would make working with ``less`` nice while simplifying our code.

What do you think? Anybody using ``less.js`` in the browser? Or there is a good reason for keeping it that I am missing?

issue_title: RFC Detailed patches and additional difference algorithms
issue_body:
For my master thesis (RFC inveniosoftware/invenio#1897) I need functionality similar to the current dictdiffer, but with the following additions
* More detailed patches
  In the current implementation, the patch extraction of insertions would generate something like this:
  ```
  {} -> {'author': {'name': 'John Doe', 'affiliation': 'CERN'}}
  ('insert', ('author',), {'name': 'John Doe', 'affiliation': 'CERN'})
  ```
  For my case, something like this would be desirable:
  ```
  ('insert', ('author',) {})
  ('insert', ('author', 'name') 'John Doe')
  ('insert', ('author', 'affiliation') 'CERN')
  ```
  Since this behavior is obviously not always wanted, there should be a way to control it.

* Different algorithms for patch extraction
  The current dictdiffer recognizes changes in list simply by their length and their content. This doesn't suit in certain situations (text represented line by line as a list).
   One alternative to this would be pythons SequenceMatcher form the difflib module, but since this also has it's quirks, a easy method to use different difference algorithms seems to be desirable.
      
Approaches
* More detailed pathes
  Since the dictdiffer is already in use, it would be a bad idea to change the default behavior to have more detailed patches, so the more reasonable approach seems to be to iterate over the patches and change them accordingly.

* Different algorithms for patch extraction
  Currently, the patch extraction for lists or dictionaries are hard coded in the diff method of the dictdiffer, but it should be easy to extract the corresponding methods.
  ```
  def diff(first, second, node=None, ignore=None, methods=[dict_method, list_method]):
      for method in methods:
          differ, intersection, addition, deletion = method(first, second, node, ignore)
              if differ:
                  break
  ```
  And when we are at it we might as well introduce a key base choice for the algorithms:
  ```
  def diff(first, second, node=None, ignore=None, methods={'default': [dict_method, list_method], <node>: special_method}):
    
      _methods = [methods.get(node)] or methods['default']
        
      for method in _methods:
          differ, intersection, addition, deletion = method(first, second, node, ignore)
              if differ:
                  break
  ```
  Even though I have to admit I don't know if this is really needed.

# UPDATE:
This issue will be closed, since the requested functionality is not needed anymore. See #53 for a more pragmatic approach.

issue_title: Cluster actions always go through the v1.2 of the API
issue_body:
When creating clusters with the `v1.3` of the API we still use the `v1.2` endpoint during cluster creation. The [`_parse_create_update`](https://github.com/qubole/qds-sdk-py/blob/master/qds_sdk/cluster.py#L114) method accepts the api-version only to distinguish the request parameters. But the [`create`](https://github.com/qubole/qds-sdk-py/blob/master/qds_sdk/cluster.py#L411), [`update`](https://github.com/qubole/qds-sdk-py/blob/master/qds_sdk/cluster.py#L419) and [`clone`](https://github.com/qubole/qds-sdk-py/blob/master/qds_sdk/cluster.py#L428) methods still use the default configuration of `Qubole.agent(..)` which is set to `v1.2` unless all the actions in the session are [`configure(..)`](https://github.com/qubole/qds-sdk-py/blob/master/qds_sdk/qubole.py#L33)`ed with the v1.3 of the API. 

In my case I want to perform some of the unsupported actions with `v1.3` of the cluster API but fall back to `v1.2` for commands APIs. One way to do this is to explicitly switch the version in the `create(..)/update(..)/clone(..)` calls which thereby alter the version for that API call alone and leave the `cached_agent` as is for the v1.2 APIs.

I'm starting a list of things people might want to configure in their Fire CLIs

- Set a function to always show --help, rather than trying to display the output.  (https://github.com/google/python-fire/issues/47)
- Prevent CLI access to private members (https://github.com/google/python-fire/issues/47)
- Prevent CLI access to specific members (https://github.com/google/python-fire/issues/47)
- Configure how a particular type is serialized
- Set a parse function for a particular input argument
- Cache a particular input to a fn
- Cache a function's output
- Set a custom help string
- Make a member available by a different name.
- Make an argument available by a different name.
-- Enable single-letter options.
- Allow passing arguments via stdin (what would be the syntax?)
- Whether to use environment variables as arguments (https://github.com/google/python-fire/issues/94)
-- What prefix to strip from environment variables (https://github.com/google/python-fire/issues/94)

**By no means do we plan on supporting all of these**; just good to keep a list.

The primary reason for making this list is so that as we start adding support for these, we can do so in a way that's forward looking. We want any configs that we introduce to look and feel consistent with each other, and not to later need to be renamed or removed for consistency with other configs added at a later date.

In Chinese, there are some words composed by an english letter and a chinese character. They should be recogized as one word during the tokenizing process. However, all the mixed-language words are splited when I use sentence piece to tokenize them. 

Is this a special feature in this tools that recognize the language before tokenizing? Can I disable this feature?

_PySequence_BytesToCharpArray contains a heap overflow after a integer wraparound occurs when large sequences are passed to subprocess_fork_exec.

This can be reproduced on a 32-bit system. It probably can be reproduced on 64bit systems with a few exabytes of RAM. (I don't have anything like that to test with unfortunately...)

```python
import subprocess32 as subprocess
subprocess._posixsubprocess.fork_exec("a",'a'*0x3FFFFFFF,"a",set(),"a","a",1,1,1,1,1,1,1,3,1,1,"a")
```
```bash
*** glibc detected *** /usr/bin/python: double free or corruption (!prev): 0x09464b38 ***
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

It can be seen in _posixsubprocess_helpers.c on line 119:

```C
    argc = PySequence_Size(self);
    if (argc == -1)
        return NULL;

array = malloc((argc + 1) * sizeof(char *));
```

argc is the size of the sequence passed to the C library. Thus, if we can pass a value such that size + 1 * 4 > 0xFFFFFFFF (0x3fffffff), malloc should return a small value, and invalid heap writes will occur in the loop below.

I was unable to trigger this through the actual unhidden API interface, so it doesn't seem too severe, but it's always possible someone else could figure out a better way to trigger the overflow. Trying to generate a valid args list that large doesn't seem possible on my system. Guessing the list overhead is too much to make this work. Passing string sequences work, but the API converts them to a list before sending them to the C library I believe.

issue_title: UnicodeDecodeError while SanitizePasswordsProcessor sanitize str keys in Py2
issue_body:
Hi all!

I have problem with this line inside `raven/processors.py:SanitizePasswordsProcessor:sanitize`:
```
key = text_type(key).lower()
```

I've got `UnicodeDecodeError`, because my key value can contain non-ascii symbols. I think it's absolutely correct, because I have a dictionary, where Django models are keys. And when this dictionary serialized to value into frames, models calls `__repr__` method, which return `str`.

That's why I think before `text_type(key)` should be explicit decode for Py2 string. I'll attach pull request later today, if you don't mind!

Thanks for your attention!

I noticed that the RDF/XML serialization is unstable, possibly yielding different results across several runs. I tried this example:

```
import rdflib

g = rdflib.Graph()
g.parse('http://xmlns.com/foaf/spec/index.rdf', format='xml')
g.serialize('/tmp/foaf1.ttl', format='turtle')
g.serialize('/tmp/foaf1.rdf', format='xml')

g = rdflib.Graph()
g.parse('http://xmlns.com/foaf/spec/index.rdf', format='xml')
g.serialize('/tmp/foaf2.ttl', format='turtle')
g.serialize('/tmp/foaf2.rdf', format='xml')
```

After running this, I ran Linux `diff` on the resulting files:
`diff /tmp/foaf1.ttl /tmp/foaf2.ttl` -> no difference
`diff /tmp/foaf1.rdf /tmp/foaf2.rdf` -> quite different

The same happens with `pretty-xml`. Can the RDF/XML serialization be made stable?

Currently, `columns` parameter is already quite versatile, allowing for multiple modes of execution. What we want is to make it also usable in the context of task #657. The following changes are expected:

- [x] If `columns` parameter is not provided, then C-side `userOverride()` function should only create an array of column names, and do not waste time composing the array of types.
- [x] If `columns` parameter is used, then instead of 2 lists (columns/types), create a single list of tuples `(name, type, format)`.
- [x] Support for 3 different callable types in `columns` should be abandoned. Instead, we will support a single callable, which will receive the array of column description tuples `(name, stype, format)`, and will in turn return an array of `bool | None | name | type | stype | (name, stype|type) | (stype|type, format) | (name, stype, format)`. This array shall be resolved at Python level and passed back as an array of `rtype | (rtype, format)` (note: support for formats may be delayed for the future).
- [x] At C level the array of rtypes should be applied to `columns`.

issue_title: "number_of_sections" in get (of both ezy and fst modules) is not correctly computed for a population
issue_body:
The "number_of_sections" should return a list of the total number of 
sections per *neuron*. Currently it adds up all the sections of the population. 
Here is an example:

```python
from neurom import fst
pop = fst.load_population('./test_data/valid_set/')
fst.get('number_of_sections', pop)

Out: array([336])
```

The number of neurons in the population are:
```python
len(pop.neurons)
Out: 5

```
So the correct result would be:

```python
Out: array([42],  [42],  [84],  [84], [84])
```

```

Currently a non-blocking server would look like this:

```python
import multiprocessing, time, threading
import msgpackrpc

# Why is this core thing buried deep in a random module?
from msgpackrpc.server import AsyncResult


def run_threaded(result, x, y):
    time.sleep(1)
    result.set_result(x + y)

class SumServer(object):
    def sum(self, x, y):
        result = AsyncResult()
        thread = threading.Thread(target=run_threaded, args=(result, x, y))
        thread.start()
        return result

def server():
    server = msgpackrpc.Server(SumServer())
    server.listen(msgpackrpc.Address("localhost", 4000))
    server.start()

def client():
    client = msgpackrpc.Client(msgpackrpc.Address("localhost", 4000))
    future = client.call_async('sum', 1, 2)
    print(future.get())

def main():
    proc_server = multiprocessing.Process(target=server)
    proc_server.start()

    time.sleep(1)

    try:
        client()
    finally:
        proc_server.terminate()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

In my opinion, this is a design problem: `AsyncResult`, which is essential to writing asynchronous servers, is contained in a non-top-level module.

There are currently two known places where inferences take place:
- In parameters values, through the `fuzzy` attribute, which artificially extends the validity period of a value.
- In some formulas, through `base_function`, which can yield values that the original requested formula could not compute on its own.
- In period management, through `calculate_output`, which can automatically sum or divide values over periods to match the requested period.
- In period management, through `set_input`, which can automatically sum or divide input values over periods to match the computable period.

We need to agree on a common, systematic way of doing inferences, to avoid conflicting views such as in #437 or in branch [`extrapolate-intensive-variables-to-past`](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/commits/extrapolate-intensive-variables-to-past).

The minimal output is a complete list of inferences and expected usage in the documentation, which could be based upon the above list.

The expected output is a set of helper functions, architectural changes and documentation that support a consistent way of inferring values in OpenFisca.

issue_title: Atomicpuppy not reading correctly from category based projections
issue_body:
When consuming events from category projections atomic puppy does not track the correct stream. 

Entries from eventstore contains two fields for the event number, one that makes reference to the original stream it was published (called `eventNumber`) and another that makes reference to the stream that is being queried (called `positionEventNumber`).

For example let's consider events that are published to the `order-{order_id}` stream and we read from the `$ce-order` stream. Let's assume and order which id 456 is placed and an event order_placed event is raised in the `order-456` stream. When reading from the `$ce-order` stream, the eventNumber field will be 0 (since is the first event published in the `order-456` stream, but it may be the third event collected in the `$ce-order` stream (because other events about other orders where published) and this the field positionEventNumber contains the correct number that is 2.

Same thing happen with the fields streamId and positionStreamId.

Extract from the json response:
```
  "entries": [
    {
      "eventId": "5f1bd258-e690-4f7c-b3dd-b46cf384871e",
      "eventType": "order_placed",
      "eventNumber": 0,
      "data": "{    \"order_id\": \"456\"}",
      "streamId": "order-456",
      "isJson": true,
      "isMetaData": false,
      "isLinkMetaData": false,

set values of a sub tensor
issue_body:
It would be great if we could have an operation (e.g., `set_subtensor`) that could set values of given indices of a tensor `X`. In theano this is implemented as [`theano.tensor.set_subtensor`](http://deeplearning.net/software/theano/library/tensor/basic.html#theano.tensor.set_subtensor).

For example: 

```
X = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]]   # tensor of shape (a, b)
idx = [1, 2]   #  1-d tensor of shape (k,) where k <= a
values = [[1, 1, 1], [2, 2, 2]]  # tensor of shape (k, b)

Y = dy.set_subtensor(X, idx, values)
Y = [[1, 2, 3], [1, 1, 1], [2, 2, 2]]
```

issue_title: Forward the whole CG without specifying a goal node
issue_body:
Hi,

is it possible to compute the value of each node in the CG at once? ie. something like cg.forward()

The current trick I use is to accumulate all the output Expression in a vector and then call forward on its sum. However this can be annoying with very big CG with independent parts.

For example, for learning with structured hinge loss, the algorithm is:
1. first, compute the weight of each part
2. use DP to compute the loss
3. call incremental_forward(loss)
4. call backward
This "bulk forward" is needed for the first step.

```
>>> rad = pyart.io.read('data/201211221820~~CONVOL:URP:WKR:RADAR:IRIS')

  File "/home/broot/scratch/miniconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyart/io/sigmet.py", line 136, in read_sigmet

Looking at line 752 in _sigmetfile.pyx, I see that it is passing in a view and an integer `nbins` to the function `_mask_gates_not_collected()`, which expects two ndarrays. Hello,  thanks for this module!

I came across a unicode problem.  You can see this works with python3:

```bash
$ python3 -c "import num2words; print(num2words.num2words(0.5, lang='de'))"
null Komma fünf null
```

but fails with python 2.7:

```bash
$ python2.7 -c "import num2words; print(num2words.num2words(0.5, lang='de'))"
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/num2words/__init__.py", line 50, in num2words
    return converter.to_cardinal(number)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/num2words/base.py", line 93, in to_cardinal
    return self.to_cardinal_float(value)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/num2words/base.py", line 127, in to_cardinal_float
    out.append(str(self.to_cardinal(curr)))
UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\xfc' in position 1: ordinal not in range(128)
```

The problem being the call to `str()` which in python3 returns a unicode string, but in 2.7 `str()` can only return ASCII bytes.  (In 2.7 `unicode()` would need to be called instead of `str()` to get a unicode string)

OpenType font has an optional table `[meta](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/typography/opentype/spec/meta)` for metadata. With this support any font built via AFDKO will be allowed to insert this table.

issue_title: PubSub: Subscriber does not propagate exception to main thread:
issue_labels: [{'id': 139685153, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20pubsub', 'name': 'api: pubsub', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438581, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20duplicate', 'name': 'status: duplicate', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'db4437', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
pubsub v0.34.0

Subscriber type: `subscribe_experimental`

# Issue
The following error usually only happens when trying to send a `ack()` or `nack()`:

```
ERROR:google.cloud.pubsub_v1.subscriber._protocol.helper_threads:ValueError: Can not send() on an RPC that has never been open()ed.
Exception in thread Thread-LeaseMaintainer:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/threading.py", line 916, in _bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/threading.py", line 864, in run
    self._target(*self._args, **self._kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/_protocol/leaser.py", line 152, in maintain_leases
    requests.ModAckRequest(ack_id, p99) for ack_id in ack_ids])
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/_protocol/dispatcher.py", line 157, in modify_ack_deadline
    self._manager.send(request)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/_protocol/streaming_pull_manager.py", line 225, in send
    self._rpc.send(request)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/_protocol/bidi.py", line 361, in send
    super(ResumableBidiRpc, self).send, request)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/_protocol/bidi.py", line 353, in _recoverable
    raise exc
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/_protocol/bidi.py", line 348, in _recoverable
    return method(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/_protocol/bidi.py", line 236, in send
    'Can not send() on an RPC that has never been open()ed.')
ValueError: Can not send() on an RPC that has never been open()ed.
```

Unfortunately the exception is only printed, but not propagated to the `future`, so the child thread dies, while the subscriber looks healthy. But it's indeed not pulling messages anymore.

# Context
When we see this happening, (or around the same time) we also see that the average time for `ack`-ing a message goes up significantly. Might blame this to network congestion, so that might hint to a loss of connection by the `rpc` client that either does not retry to connect or it just can't, and fails doing so. But in such case the appropriate error should be raised and surfaced. In fact even having several instances of the subscriber, when this incident happens, it happens pretty much at the same time across all the instances. A network problem would explain why all instances tend to error at the same time, with the same error.

There is a chance that this issue is similar or somehow related to this other one: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/5306

# Details
python version: 3.6.3
## pubsub packages:
google-api-core==1.1.2
google-auth==1.4.1
google-cloud-pubsub==0.34.0
googleapis-common-protos==1.5.3
grpc-google-iam-v1==0.11.4
grpcio==1.12.0

issue_title: `pex -c` does not work for multiplatform pexes when the target dist is platform specific
issue_body:
If the console script is housed in a dist that is platform-specific and the pex is multiplatform, then the same script is found more than once and pex creation fails with something like:
```
RuntimeError: Ambiguous script specification pants matches multiple entry points:pants = pants.bin.pants_loader:main pants = pants.bin.pants_loader:main
```
Pex should probably allow dups from a single requirement key if the found scripts are bytewise identical as they are in this example case.

See https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/6349 for an example of getting bit by this.

- Minimum setuptools version should be 20.3 (#391)
- Improve wheel support in pex. (#388)

issue_title: Switch to using AnalysisMappers to rewrite zinc analysis
issue_body: As mentioned on https://github.com/sbt/zinc/issues/218, the AnalysisMappers class allows for on-the-fly rewrites of analysis, would allow it to be rebased as it is saved to disk, to avoid the additional decoding/rebasing pass that we currently execute with zincutils.

I think this means the [`Fetcher`](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/net/http/fetcher.py#L21) should support them out of the box.

Since we don't support this today, you have to do more work than you should to depend on local binaries (or binaries mounted via NFS for example). See here for an example of the extra work (Context is Aurora): https://gist.github.com/jsirois/f4ea2167f397e544549edbdea005581c

The reporting server's index includes 2 entries for each pants run. The upper entry is for the pantsd portion and the lower entry is for the thin client portion.

If you click on the pantsd one, ie the top one, only the pants command that was run is printed. The timing data is missing.

If you click on the thin client one, the server crashes, but does not log how it crashed. From what I can tell, it doesn't even write to a log file in the work directory.

I turned off the daemon, and got a stacktrace.

```
Exception happened during processing of request from ('127.0.0.1', 52156)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../python/2.7/lib/python2.7/SocketServer.py", line 295, in _handle_request_noblock
    self.process_request(request, client_address)
  File ".../python/2.7/lib/python2.7/SocketServer.py", line 321, in process_request

issue_title: ProjectTree reports files in OS-specific orders
issue_body:
ProjectTree `scandir` currently reports files in an OS/fs-specific order, as evidenced by https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/4121/ : this will likely lead to `Snapshot` ID mismatches across hosts, and other nasty inconsistencies.

I think that the right fix would be to provide an ordering guarantee at the ProjectTree level by sorting the resulting entries, and then validate/enforce it in [scan_directory](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/engine/fs.py#L256).

issue_title: The trace for an error in a context with a lot of dependents is redundant
issue_body:
The `Graph.trace` for a cycle detected in a context where the failed/cyclic Node has a lot of dependents is very redundant. See @ericzundel 's response to my question on https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/4087/ for an example.

To repro, introduce a failure inside of a graph, and then list a target spec that covers a bunch of things that depend on it... ie: break guava in 3rdparty, and then attempt to run `./pants list ::`.

Most likely what we want to do is invert the trace: rather than rendering downward from the roots, we should discover all failed nodes, and then, for each node, choose some number of relevant paths (or just one: the shortest...?) to the root to render.

issue_title: Support finer control for assigning fetchers to remotes.
issue_body:
The rough sketch idea is 2 part:
1. Introduce `[fetchers] fetchers` which is a new dict config of name (will be used as an options scope) -> FetcherType, eg:
```ini
[fetchers]
fetchers: {
    'artifactory.com': 'ArchiveFetcher',
    'github.com': 'ArchiveFetcher'
  }
```
The existing `[fetchers] mapping` becomes an ordered mapping from regex to fetcher name, eg:
```ini
[fetchers]
mapping: [
    ('github.com/gorilla', 'github.com'),
    ('.*', 'artifactory.com')
  ]
```
The ordering allows a dev to put mappings for the new stuff they want to try at the top of the list.

The Fetchers class would then instantiate Fetchers that are Subsystems using their names as their option scope.  This allows having 2 `ArchiveFetcher` instances configured differently - one pointed at github.com, one at artifactory.

This grew out of discussion here: https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2655/

issue_title: Ability to add the same collector with different labels/tags
issue_body:
At the moment, the CollectorRegistry is unable to accept the same metric with different labels/tags because the following error pops up.
`Duplicated timeseries in CollectorRegistry: set([u'my_metric'])`
This is due to the fact that metric duplication is defined based on the metric name and not on the tags/labels as well, [here](https://github.com/prometheus/client_python/blob/ec1a9f66896d019c05f2c686311965306eaa62e3/prometheus_client/core.py#L55)

This prevents the push gateway from having the same metric with multiple tags/labels 
my_metric [my_common_tag='some_value', `my_different_tag='value_1'`]
my_metric [my_common_tag='some_value', `my_different_tag='value_2'` ]

Right now, the only way to accommodate this use case is to insert a discriminator in the name of the metric.
my_metric_`my_different_tag_value_1` [my_common_tag='something']
my_metric_`my_different_tag_value_2` [my_common_tag='something']

But this is very bad for applying aggregation functions on top

issue_title: some Korean-English names fail to parse
issue_body:
I have a large data set, which includes many Koreans with Korean names living in the United States. The names are purely written in Latin characters, but sometimes the parser fails to return anything at all. It seems to be confused by Korean surnames that are often European first names (e.g., Lee, Kim) and less common names.

I cannot send you my private data set, but after speaking with a colleague who is a native Korean speaker, we came up with a procedure to generate a synthetic data set. It seems like what you do with Census data.

Procedure
1.	Create a list of two-part given names from [this table on Wikipedia ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Korean_given_names) by combining all the first syllables (the rows) with the second syllables (the columns). Example: Ki Nam
2. Combine the two-part given names with three common patterns. For example, Ki Nam can be separated by space (Ki Nam), the first two parts combined (Kinam), or the first two parts combined by hyphen (Ki-nam). Technically in this example Ki Nam is the given name, and Name is not the middle name.
3.	Add the top surnames from fifth column ("popular spellings") from [this table on Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_South_Korean_surnames_by_prevalence)

Koreans spell their English name sometimes with the surname first (e.g., Kim Ki Nam) or last (e.g., Ki Nam Kim), but I assume the former is too confusing for the parser, so for now I would focus on the latter.

Examples: Ki Nam Kim, Kinam Kim, Ki-Nam Lee

The full procedure would produce hundreds of examples. Is some quantity too many, or is more always better?

issue_title: Didl class for Sonos favorites - code style
issue_body:
To make browsing Sonos favorites possible with the existing music library methods, a data structure for the `object.itemobject.item.sonos_favorite` Didl class is required.

I have created this class, but I require your advice for code style:
In the metadata for a Sonos favorite, there is a `<resMD>` tag which contains the metadata of the item to which the favorite refers. To make its contents accessible to the user, there are two possible implementations:

1. When the DidlFavorite object is created, parse the resMD metadata and make the referenced object available via an attribute which can be accessed and modified. When the favorite it is converted back to XML, call `to_didl_string()` on the referenced object to get the metadata back.
This can be archieved by adding a `resource_meta_data` property - see [`ghcs27:favorite_data_structure`](https://github.com/ghcs27/SoCo/tree/favorite_data_structure.)
2. Keep the resMD metadata in its own attribute. Make it accessible by adding a `reference` property which parses the metadata on read. For this, see [`ghcs27:favorite_data_structure_alt`](https://github.com/ghcs27/SoCo/tree/favorite_data_structure_alt.)

Which implementation do you prefer?
